content:
2 min read
5 hours ago

--

Angular Signals aren’t the future.
They’re the present and they’re quietly changing how we write apps.
If you’re still using RxJS for every tiny reactive need, you’re making your life harder.
Signals won’t replace RxJS completely but they will replace a big chunk of unnecessary observable boilerplate that slows you down.
Today, let’s break down 7 real-world Signals patterns that will instantly make your Angular code cleaner, faster, and easier to reason about.
1. Component State Without Subjects
Old way:
loading$ = new BehaviorSubject(false);New way:
loading = signal(false);No subscriptions.
No BehaviorSubject clutter.
Just clean, reactive state.
2. Derived Data Without map()
In RxJS, you transform streams.
With Signals, you derive values:
items = signal([1, 2, 3]);
count = computed(() => items().length);
